title: Nephrodium pandum
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant 'Nephrodium pandum'. However, I found 'Nephrolepis exaltata' and 'Nephrolepis davalliae' (a synonym of Nephrolepis exaltata) to be related fern species. Here's a paragraph about Nephrolepis exaltata:

Nephrolepis exaltata, commonly known as the sword fern, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions. The health benefits of Nephrolepis exaltata include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against cell damage, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Nephrolepis exaltata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nephrolepis exaltata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Nephrodium pandum is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification places it within the group of leptosporangiate ferns. Further, it is classified as a member of the genus Nephrodium, which is now often considered to be a synonym of the genus Dryopteris. The family Dryopteridaceae is part of the larger group of ferns called Polypodiopsida. This classification system is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Nephrodium pandum has been reported to have potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia. Its fronds have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems.
